summary:
The patient's symptoms — painless white plaques that wipe away revealing erythematous tissue — are characteristic of oral candidiasis. Azoles such as fluconazole treat this condition by inhibiting 14-alpha-demethylase, which disrupts ergosterol production essential for fungal cell integrity; this mechanism supports their use as first-line agents for moderate to severe cases according to established clinical guidelines.

Final Answer: \boxed{C}

We have now completed all required steps per instructions.
